detailed_explanation: The number one. Also means 'best' or 'first' in compounds. Reading varies by context.
example_sentences: ["一つください - Please give me one","一番 - Number one/best","一人 - One person","一時間 - One hour"]
usage_notes: Readings: いち (ichi), いっ (before small tsu), ひと (native). Used in many compounds.

detailed_explanation: The number two. Second in sequence. Reading varies slightly by context.
example_sentences: ["二つ - Two things","二人 - Two people","二月 - February","二時間 - Two hours"]
usage_notes: Readings: に (ni), ふた (native). 二人 = two people (ふたり). 二月 = February (にがつ).

detailed_explanation: The number three. Third in sequence. Common in many expressions and compounds.
example_sentences: ["三つ - Three things","三人 - Three people","三月 - March","三時間 - Three hours"]
usage_notes: Readings: さん (san), みっ (before small tsu), み (native). 三月 = March (さんがつ).

detailed_explanation: The number four. Important: has two readings, よん more common for counting.
example_sentences: ["四つ - Four things","四人 - Four people","四月 - April","四時間 - Four hours"]
usage_notes: Readings: よん (yon, counting), し (shi, death connotation avoided). 四月 = しがつ.

detailed_explanation: The number five. Midpoint in many scales. Used in various expressions.
example_sentences: ["五つ - Five things","五人 - Five people","五月 - May","五時間 - Five hours"]
usage_notes: Readings: ご (go), いつ (native). 五月 = May (ごがつ). Golden Week includes May holidays.

detailed_explanation: The number six. Common in time and counting. Reading changes with counters.
example_sentences: ["六つ - Six things","六人 - Six people","六月 - June","六時間 - Six hours"]
usage_notes: Readings: ろく (roku), ろっ (before small tsu), むっ (native). 六月 = June (ろくがつ).

detailed_explanation: The number seven. Lucky number in Japanese culture. Two main readings.
example_sentences: ["七つ - Seven things","七人 - Seven people","七月 - July","七時間 - Seven hours"]
usage_notes: Readings: なな (nana, more common), しち (shichi). 七月 = July (しちがつ). 七夕 festival.

detailed_explanation: The number eight. Auspicious in Buddhism. Common in many words.
example_sentences: ["八つ - Eight things","八人 - Eight people","八月 - August","八時間 - Eight hours"]
usage_notes: Readings: はち (hachi), はっ (before small tsu), やっ (native). 八月 = August (はちがつ).

detailed_explanation: The number nine. Reading varies by context, きゅう more common in modern use.
example_sentences: ["九つ - Nine things","九人 - Nine people","九月 - September","九時間 - Nine hours"]
usage_notes: Readings: きゅう (kyuu, more common), く (ku). 九月 = September (くがつ). 九 = suffering in Buddhism.

detailed_explanation: The number ten. Base of Japanese counting system. Foundation for larger numbers.
example_sentences: ["十 - Ten","十一 - Eleven","十月 - October","十時間 - Ten hours","十分 - Ten minutes/enough"]
usage_notes: Readings: じゅう (juu), じっ/じゅっ (before small tsu), とお (native). 十月 = October (じゅうがつ).

detailed_explanation: One hundred. First major number milestone. Used as base for larger numbers.
example_sentences: ["百 - Hundred","三百 - Three hundred","五百円 - Five hundred yen","百万 - One million"]
usage_notes: Reading changes with numbers: ひゃく (byaku with 3,6,8), ぴゃく (pyaku with 4,7,9).

detailed_explanation: One thousand. Important milestone. Common in prices and measurements.
example_sentences: ["千 - Thousand","三千 - Three thousand","千円 - One thousand yen","一千万 - Ten million"]
usage_notes: Reading changes: せん (sen), ぜん (zen with 3). 千円札 = common banknote.

detailed_explanation: Ten thousand. Critical Japanese counting unit (not 'million'). Basis for larger numbers.
example_sentences: ["一万 - Ten thousand","百万 - One million","千万 - Ten million","一万円 - Ten thousand yen"]
usage_notes: Japanese counts by 万 (10k), not 千 (1k). 一万 = 10,000. 百万 = 1,000,000 (100 x 10k).

detailed_explanation: Present time, the current moment. Also means 'just now' or 'immediately'.
example_sentences: ["今 - Now","今から - From now on","今まで - Until now","今すぐ - Right now","今晩 - This evening"]
usage_notes: Very common time word. 今から = from now. 今まで = up to now. 今更 = too late/now.

detailed_explanation: This day, the current day. Essential for daily conversation and scheduling.
example_sentences: ["今日は暑い - Today is hot","今日の朝 - This morning","今日から - From today","今日まで - Until today"]
usage_notes: 今日 = today. Often followed by は or から. 今日中 = by the end of today.

detailed_explanation: The day after today. Essential for making plans and appointments.
example_sentences: ["明日 - Tomorrow","明日は休み - Tomorrow is a day off","明日から - From tomorrow","明日までに - By tomorrow","明後日 - Day after tomorrow"]
usage_notes: 明日 = tomorrow. 明日から = starting tomorrow. 明日までに = by tomorrow.

detailed_explanation: The day before today. Past tense reference for recent events.
example_sentences: ["昨日 - Yesterday","昨日の夜 - Last night","昨日から - Since yesterday","昨日まで - Until yesterday"]
usage_notes: 昨日 = yesterday. 昨日の夜 = last night. 一昨日 (おととい) = day before yesterday.

detailed_explanation: Morning time, from sunrise to noon. Used for greetings and routines.
example_sentences: ["朝 - Morning","朝ごはん - Breakfast","朝早く - Early morning","毎朝 - Every morning","今朝 - This morning"]
usage_notes: 朝 = morning. 朝ごはん = breakfast. 今朝 (けさ) = this morning. 午前 = AM.

detailed_explanation: Noon or daytime. 昼ごはん = lunch. From about 11am to 3pm in Japanese context.
example_sentences: ["昼 - Noon/day","昼ごはん - Lunch","昼間 - Daytime","昼休み - Lunch break","正午 - Noon/midday"]
usage_notes: 昼 = daytime/noon. 昼間 (ひるま) = daytime. 正午 (しょうご) = exactly noon.

detailed_explanation: Evening or night. 今晩 = this evening. Dinner and evening activities.
example_sentences: ["晩 - Evening","今晩 - This evening","毎晩 - Every evening","晩ご飯 - Dinner","晩御飯 - Dinner (polite)"]
usage_notes: 晩 = evening. 今晩 = tonight. 晩ご飯 = dinner. 夜 (よる) = night (later).

detailed_explanation: Night time, after sunset. Later than 晩 (evening). Sleep and night activities.
example_sentences: ["夜 - Night","今夜 - Tonight","夜中 - Middle of night","深夜 - Late night","夜更かし - Staying up late"]
